method_card.parent_question: How can long-context LLM methods extract reusable trading judgment from messy trader text?
intended_inputs: same selected second-source excerpt as chunked_source_grounding pilots 004, 005, and 006: the operator-approved local pharm box-trades excerpt
metadata-safe source hash and source ref for the selected excerpt
the line-range-first locator lesson from the chunked_source_grounding Goal 13A decision review
DeepSeek V4 Pro with thinking disabled
pre-recorded prompt and model configuration metadata
intended_outputs: judgment principle proposals with supporting claim references
locally computed quote hashes from validated spans
known_risks: This is a proposed live LLM pilot planning record, not a completed run.
Requiring line-range support may flatten broader judgment back into narrow claims, repeating the limited_abstraction tradeoff instead of resolving it.
Broader abstraction may revert to generic advice or over_abstracted_teacher_intent when grounding pressure is added.
The combined contract is larger than pilot 006's contract and may not fit the output cap, repeating the chunked pilot 001 contract-size failure.
The model may still emit line ranges that are broad or nearby rather than support-valid.
The model must not emit character offsets; local review computes offsets only after accepting the line range.
The model must not be trusted to generate cryptographic hashes; quote hashes are computed locally only from validated spans.
Prompt or trace handling may accidentally include raw source material if the privacy boundary is not followed.
non_goals: not a replacement for the chunked_source_grounding method family
